summary: Full E2E testing of TaskSync (FastAPI + MongoDB + React). All 12 backend pytest cases pass (health, auth, projects CRUD, tasks CRUD with status transitions, error handling for invalid project_id and invalid task_id). All frontend flows pass via Playwright: Login screen with glassmorphic card, role toggle, Member login hides Admin Panel, Admin login shows Admin Panel, role badge renders correctly (ADMIN/MEMBER), all 8+ seeded task cards render with project_name/assignee/due/status/priority, task modal opens and shows 3 status update buttons, status update reflects via toast and persists, search filter narrows results, logout returns to login, create project as Admin updates the project dropdown, create task as Admin appears in Pending column. No console errors. Mock auth confirmed (any creds accepted).
critical_code_review_comments: FastAPI deprecation: uses @app.on_event('startup') / 'shutdown' which is deprecated in newer FastAPI versions; recommend migrating to the lifespan context manager.

Mock auth: token is just a uuid string and is never validated server-side. Acceptable since the task specifies mock auth, but no protected endpoints exist either; if protection is added later, add a dependency that validates Authorization header.
Drag-and-drop not exercised by automated tests (hello-pangea/dnd is hard to script reliably); UI implementation looks correct but relies on visual verification.
No DELETE endpoint surfaced in the UI even though backend supports it (minor).
test_credentials: Mock auth - any username/password works. Used: maya/demo123 (Admin), diego/demo123 (Member).
seed_data_creation: Backend seeds 3 projects + 8 tasks on startup automatically. Tests created TEST_-prefixed projects/tasks; tasks were cleaned up, test projects remain (no DELETE projects endpoint).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend endpoints verified via /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (12/12 passing). All frontend critical flows verified via Playwright. Drag-and-drop with @hello-pangea/dnd was not automated due to library complexity but the modal-based status update path covers the same handler (handleStatusChange). REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL works through /api prefix. MongoDB seeding is idempotent (count_documents check).
